explanation:
The requirement can be split into three core conditions for LTL:
1. If in PCV or PSV modes and if the inspiratory pause is set by the GUI (inspiratoryPauseButton), the system shall, after the next end of inspiration (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), if the inspiratory pause is still required (inspiratoryPauseButton holds), close both inValve and outValve, and maintain this until the pause is stopped by the GUI (¬inspiratoryPauseButton).

To capture this formally:
A. "In PCV or PSV mode" is (PCVMode | PSVMode).
B. "Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI" is inspiratoryPauseButton.
C. When inspiratoryPauseButton is pressed under these modes, after the next inspiratoryPhaseEnd, if the button remains pressed (inspiratoryPauseButton), both inValveClose and outValveClose should hold ('until' the button is released, i.e. while inspiratoryPauseButton holds). 

--- Full Structure ---
Whenever (PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton becomes true, then after the next inspiratoryPhaseEnd, as long as inspiratoryPauseButton holds, both inValveClose & outValveClose hold, until inspiratoryPauseButton becomes false.
We encode this causality for every time the inspiratoryPauseButton is pressed in the proper mode:
G(
  ((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton & X(!inspiratoryPauseButton U inspiratoryPhaseEnd))
  ->
  X(
    inspiratoryPhaseEnd 
      ->
        ( (inspiratoryPauseButton) -> ((inValveClose & outValveClose) U !inspiratoryPauseButton ))
    )
  )
)
However, an alternative, simpler LTL version can be:
G(
  (((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton & X(F inspiratoryPhaseEnd))
    ->
      F( inspiratoryPhaseEnd & X((inspiratoryPauseButton) -> G((inspiratoryPauseButton) -> (inValveClose & outValveClose)) U !inspiratoryPauseButton ) ) 
    )
)
But the first encoding is more directly traceable. We proceed with that.

When Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI under these modes, after the end of the next inspiration, if Inspiratory Pause still required, both inspiratory and expiratory valves will close until inspiratory pause is stopped by the GUI

output_LTL: G( (((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton & X(!inspiratoryPauseButton U inspiratoryPhaseEnd)) -> X( inspiratoryPhaseEnd -> ( (inspiratoryPauseButton) -> ((inValveClose & outValveClose) U !inspiratoryPauseButton )) )))

explanation: The sentence describes a requirement in PCV and PSV ventilator modes: If the user sets inspiratoryPauseButton, at the end of the next inspiration phase (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), if inspiratoryPauseButton is still set, both inValveClose (inspiratory valve close) and outValveClose (expiratory valve close) must hold until the user stops the inspiratory pause (¬inspiratoryPauseButton). This is interpreted globally (G) and only relevant in PCVMode or PSVMode. So, for every time the inspiratoryPauseButton becomes set, after the next inspiratoryPhaseEnd, if inspiratoryPauseButton is still set, both valves stay closed until inspiratoryPauseButton is unset. The main building blocks: 1) (PCVMode | PSVMode) – system is in one of the modes; 2) inspiratoryPauseButton – user sets inspiratory pause on GUI; 3) inspiratoryPhaseEnd – end of an inspiration phase; 4) inValveClose & outValveClose – both valves are closed; 5) inspiratoryPauseButton is still set until it is unset (¬inspiratoryPauseButton). The LTL formula encodes: globally, in PCV or PSV, when inspiratoryPauseButton is set, at the next inspiratoryPhaseEnd, if it remains set, both valves close continuously until inspiratoryPauseButton is unset.

if Inspiratory Pause still required, both valves close until pause is stopped by GUI
Globally, when inspiratoryPauseButton becomes set, after next inspiratoryPhaseEnd, if still set, close valves until button is unset

explanation: First, the condition applies in both PCV and PSV modes: so (PCVMode | PSVMode) holds. 1) If the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI ('inspiratoryPauseButton'), the ventilator must (a) wait until the end of the next inspiration phase ('inspiratoryPhaseEnd'), and (b) if the inspiratory pause is still required, both the inspiratory and expiratory valves are closed ('inValveClose' & 'outValveClose'), (c) until the inspiratory pause is stopped (i.e., while 'inspiratoryPauseButton' is true). This requires: Whenever (globally) we have (PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton, then after the next inspiratoryPhaseEnd (using a temporal F after the setting), as long as inspiratoryPauseButton holds, inValveClose & outValveClose must be true, until the button is released (i.e., !inspiratoryPauseButton). This can be formalized in LTL as: G((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton -> (F(inspiratoryPhaseEnd) & X(F((inspiratoryPauseButton) -> ((inValveClose & outValveClose) U (!inspiratoryPauseButton)))))). But, since 'wait for the end of the next inspiration phase' means, only after the next end, the valves must close if pause required. So, a better structure is: G((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton -> F( inspiratoryPhaseEnd & X (inspiratoryPauseButton -> ( (inValveClose & outValveClose) U (!inspiratoryPauseButton) ) ) ) ). That is: globally, if we are in the correct modes and inspiratory pause is set, then eventually the next inspiration phase will end, and then, as long as the pause is required, both valves are closed, until the pause is stopped by the GUI.

explanation:
We are asked to formalize the behavior in PCV and PSV modes regarding the Inspiratory Pause function. The key requirements are: (1) If the Inspiratory Pause button is set in GUI, then after the end of the next inspiration phase, AND if the pause is still required, both valves (inValveClose and outValveClose) close, and stay closed until inspiratoryPauseButton is unset.

Step-by-step translation:
- Being in PCV or PSV mode: PCVMode | PSVMode
- Inspiratory Pause is set by GUI: inspiratoryPauseButton
- Wait for end of next inspiration phase: (at some point after inspiratoryPauseButton, inspiratoryPhaseEnd)
- If pause is still required (inspiratoryPauseButton still set): inspiratoryPauseButton
- Both valves closed: inValveClose & outValveClose
- Until inspiratoryPauseButton is unset: (inValveClose & outValveClose) holds UNTIL NOT inspiratoryPauseButton

That is, whenever the pause button is set, after the next end of inspiration, if the pause button is still set, then both valves shall close and remain closed until the pause button is released. Because we can set the pause button any time during PCV or PSV, we universally (globally) require for all such events.

High-level formula:
Globally, if in PCV or PSV mode and inspiratoryPauseButton becomes true, then after the end of the next inspiration phase, IF the button is still true, then both valves are closed UNTIL the button is released.

To express this, we monitor for inspiratoryPauseButton becoming true (using F to look for the next inspiratoryPhaseEnd), then require (inValveClose & outValveClose) until NOT inspiratoryPauseButton.

Complete LTL formula:
G( ((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton & X(F inspiratoryPhaseEnd & X inspiratoryPauseButton)) -> X(F (inspiratoryPhaseEnd & X (inspiratoryPauseButton & G((inValveClose & outValveClose) U !inspiratoryPauseButton)))) )

(We move one step after inspiratoryPhaseEnd, check still inspiratoryPauseButton, and then require closure of both valves as long as the button is held.)

Both valves remain closed while pause is active
All above holds globally

explanation:
Let's break the requirement into LTL components:

1. The phrase 'in PCV and PSV modes there shall be the possibility to initiate an Inspiratory Pause if it is set by the GUI' says that the system should allow the inspiratory pause to be initiated while in either PCVMode or PSVMode, and if inspiratoryPauseButton is set.

2. 'When the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI, the ventilator shall wait for the end of the next inspiration phase': This says that, upon inspiratoryPauseButton, you wait for the next occurrence of inspiratoryPhaseEnd.

3. 'If the Inspiratory Pause still required, both the inspiratory and expiratory valves will close until the inspiratory pause is stopped by the GUI': After inspiratoryPhaseEnd, if inspiratoryPauseButton (or inspiratoryPause) is still true, then inValveClose and outValveClose must hold until inspiratoryPauseButton is no longer true (i.e., until the inspiratory pause is stopped by the GUI).

Globally, whenever we're in (PCVMode or PSVMode), and inspiratoryPauseButton becomes true, then after the next inspiratoryPhaseEnd, as long as the pause is required (inspiratoryPauseButton remains true), inValveClose and outValveClose must hold, until inspiratoryPauseButton is false.

We can structure this as:
1. G( (PCVMode | PSVMode) & X( inspiratoryPauseButton ) ->
2.     F( inspiratoryPhaseEnd & X( inspiratoryPauseButton -> (inValveClose & outValveClose U !inspiratoryPauseButton) )))

Alternatively, to represent that after the 'next' inspiratoryPhaseEnd after pause is requested, you must enforce the valve closing as above, the core implication is:
G( (PCVMode|PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton & X( !(inspiratoryPhaseEnd & X inspiratoryPauseButton )) U (inspiratoryPhaseEnd & X inspiratoryPauseButton) ->
G( inValveClose & outValveClose U !inspiratoryPauseButton ) )

But a more readable/linear approach is:
Globally, if in PCVMode or PSVMode, and inspiratoryPauseButton becomes true, then after next inspiratoryPhaseEnd, as long as inspiratoryPauseButton holds, inValveClose and outValveClose must hold, until the button is released.
Thus:
G( (PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton & X( F( inspiratoryPhaseEnd )) -> F( inspiratoryPhaseEnd & X( inspiratoryPauseButton -> ( (inValveClose & outValveClose) U !inspiratoryPauseButton  ))) )

For simplicity, we will represent: Globally, whenever (PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton, then after F(inspiratoryPhaseEnd), if inspiratoryPauseButton is still held, then (inValveClose & outValveClose) must hold until the pause is no longer requested.

Final LTL: G( (PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton -> F( inspiratoryPhaseEnd & X (inspiratoryPauseButton -> (inValveClose & outValveClose) U (!inspiratoryPauseButton)) ))

explanation:
Let us break down the requirements step by step: 
1. The feature applies only in PCV and PSV modes: (PCVMode | PSVMode).
2. The 'possibility to initiate an Inspiratory Pause if it is set by the GUI': when inspiratoryPauseButton is set by the GUI.
3. The ventilator shall wait for the end of the next inspiration phase: after inspiratoryPauseButton is pressed, wait for inspiratoryPhaseEnd.
4. If the Inspiratory Pause is still required (i.e., the GUI button is still on), both the inspiratory and expiratory valves will close: inspiratoryPauseButton & inValveClose & outValveClose.
5. This situation (both valves closed) holds 'until the inspiratory pause is stopped by the GUI' (i.e., until not inspiratoryPauseButton).
Therefore, whenever in PCV or PSV mode, and if inspiratoryPauseButton is pressed, after the next inspiratoryPhaseEnd, as long as inspiratoryPauseButton remains true, both inValveClose and outValveClose remain true, until inspiratoryPauseButton becomes false.
This overall scenario has a trigger (inspiratoryPauseButton), a wait (for inspiratoryPhaseEnd), then an invariant (valves closed) as long as inspiratoryPauseButton is still true, and an exit condition (inspiratoryPauseButton released). LTL for 'after A, then B holds while C' is G(A -> X(F(Some B start condition) -> (C U ~A))).
We'll use auxiliary naming:
Trigger = inspiratoryPauseButton
Wait = inspiratoryPhaseEnd
Hold = inValveClose & outValveClose
End = !inspiratoryPauseButton

So in LTL: 
Globally, whenever we're in PCV or PSV mode and inspiratoryPauseButton is pressed, after the next inspiratoryPhaseEnd, if inspiratoryPauseButton is still pressed, both valves will be closed until inspiratoryPauseButton is released.
Translated: G(((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton) -> (X(F inspiratoryPhaseEnd -> X((inspiratoryPauseButton -> ((inValveClose & outValveClose) U !inspiratoryPauseButton))))))

Wait for end of the next inspiration phase
If Inspiratory Pause still set, both valves are closed until Inspiratory Pause is stopped
Globally, whenever the above triggers, the property must hold after the wait

explanation:
First, the requirement is: In PCV and PSV modes, if an Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI, then at the end of the next inspiration phase, if the Inspiratory Pause is still required, both the inspiratory and expiratory valves shall close until the pause is stopped by the GUI.

Let: PCVMode and PSVMode be the modes; inspiratoryPauseButton means the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI; inspiratoryPhaseEnd marks the end of inspiration; inspiratoryPause means the pause is still required; inValveClose and outValveClose mean both valves are closed. The pause is stopped by the GUI when !inspiratoryPauseButton holds (assuming the pause ends when the button is released/unset).

Step 1: 'In PCV and PSV modes there shall be the possibility to initiate an Inspiratory Pause if it is set by the GUI.' This means the rule holds only when PCVMode or PSVMode holds.

Step 2: 'When the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI,' maps to inspiratoryPauseButton being true.

Step 3: 'the ventilator shall wait for the end of the next inspiration phase...' means, starting from the trigger, at the next occurrence of inspiratoryPhaseEnd (the next end of inspiration).

Step 4: 'if the Inspiratory Pause still required,' maps to inspiratoryPauseButton still being true at that future time.

Step 5: 'both the inspiratory and expiratory valves will close until the inspiratory pause is stopped by the GUI.' This means from that point, inValveClose & outValveClose must hold until !inspiratoryPauseButton.

The overall requirement is:
"Globally, if we are in PCV or PSV mode and inspiratoryPauseButton becomes true, then after the next inspiratoryPhaseEnd, if inspiratoryPauseButton is still true, then (inValveClose & outValveClose) holds until inspiratoryPauseButton becomes false."

This is commonly written as:
G((PCVMode | PSVMode) & X F (inspiratoryPauseButton) -> X (F (inspiratoryPhaseEnd & X inspiratoryPauseButton & X ((inValveClose & outValveClose) U (!inspiratoryPauseButton)))))

But with a more targeted spec: On every rising edge of inspiratoryPauseButton in PCV or PSV, after the next inspiratoryPhaseEnd, if pause still required, ensure inValveClose & outValveClose until pause stops.

This can be simplified using LTL triggers:
G( ((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton & X (!inspiratoryPauseButton)) -> 
    X(F( inspiratoryPhaseEnd & X (inspiratoryPauseButton & (inValveClose & outValveClose) U (!inspiratoryPauseButton)) ))
)

But since you want to state: Whenever inspiratoryPauseButton is active, at the next inspiratoryPhaseEnd, if pause still required, then both valves must close and stay closed until pause stops.

So the formula is:
G( ((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton) -> F( inspiratoryPhaseEnd & X( inspiratoryPauseButton -> ((inValveClose & outValveClose) U (!inspiratoryPauseButton)))))

Where:
- G(...): globally
- (PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton: in correct mode and GUI pause requested
- F(...): at some future point (the next inspiratoryPhaseEnd)
- X(...): in the state after inspiratoryPhaseEnd
- inspiratoryPauseButton -> ((inValveClose & outValveClose) U (!inspiratoryPauseButton)): if pause still required, keep valves closed until pause stops

the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI

both inspiratory and expiratory valves will close until pause is stopped
globally

explanation:
We first interpret the specification and break down the relevant semantics: (1) Inspiratory Pause can be initiated in PCV or PSV mode if set by GUI (inspiratoryPauseButton). (2) When inspiratoryPauseButton becomes true, the ventilator waits for the end of the next inspiration phase. (3) If inspiratoryPauseButton is *still* true right after that inspiration phase ends, inspiratoryPause should be activated (both valves closed: inValveClose & outValveClose), and this holds until the pause is stopped (inspiratoryPauseButton becomes false).

To express this in LTL, we interpret (a) "When inspiratoryPauseButton becomes true": G(inspiratoryPauseButton -> ...); (b) "wait for next inspiratoryPhaseEnd": this is a temporal delay after inspiratoryPauseButton becomes set; (c) "if still required": inspiratoryPauseButton remains true at inspiratoryPhaseEnd; (d) "both valves closed until the inspiratory pause is stopped": inValveClose & outValveClose holds globally until inspiratoryPauseButton becomes false.

This is a complex trigger-on-event requirement. We capture the essential behaviour in the following LTL:

\nG( (PCVMode | PSVMode) & X(inspiratoryPauseButton & X(F (inspiratoryPhaseEnd & inspiratoryPauseButton))) -> F [ (inspiratoryPhaseEnd & inspiratoryPauseButton) -> ( (inValveClose & outValveClose) U (!inspiratoryPauseButton) ) ] )

But in LTL, to be precise and structured, we describe: Whenever, in PCV or PSV mode, the inspiratoryPauseButton is set (becomes true), eventually after the next inspiratoryPhaseEnd occurs with the button still true, then both inValve and outValve remain closed until the button becomes false (pause stopped).

Shortened to core logic for clarity:
\nG( ((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton & X(F (inspiratoryPhaseEnd & inspiratoryPauseButton))) -> F ( (inspiratoryPhaseEnd & inspiratoryPauseButton) -> ( (inValveClose & outValveClose) U !inspiratoryPauseButton ) ) )

Main LTL: G( ((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton) -> F ( (inspiratoryPhaseEnd & inspiratoryPauseButton) -> ( (inValveClose & outValveClose) U !inspiratoryPauseButton ) ) )

This expresses:
- If the system is in PCV or PSV mode and inspiratoryPauseButton is set, then there will eventually be a moment after the end of inspiration while the button is still set, after which both valves stay closed until the pause ends.

explanation_dict.natural_language: System is in PCV or PSV mode
The inspiratory pause is requested (button set)
After inspiratoryPauseButton, after the end of the next inspiration phase (inspiratoryPhaseEnd) with button still set...
When inspiratoryPhaseEnd happens and the button is still set, both valves remain closed until the pause is cancelled (button reset)
Globally: Whenever in PCV or PSV mode and button set, eventually after the next inspiratory phase ended and button still set, valves are closed until pause is stopped.
explanation_dict.LTL: G( ((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton) -> F ( (inspiratoryPhaseEnd & inspiratoryPauseButton) -> ( (inValveClose & outValveClose) U !inspiratoryPauseButton ) ) )

explanation:
First, let's extract and translate the requirements from the natural language:

1. The modes of interest are called PCVMode and PSVMode. The possibility to initiate an Inspiratory Pause is checked when inspiratoryPauseButton is pressed.
2. When inspiratoryPauseButton is set, at the end of the next inspiration phase (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), and if inspiratoryPauseButton is still pressed, then both inValveClose and outValveClose must be true (both valves stay closed) until the pause is stopped (inspiratoryPauseButton is released).

Breaking down step by step:
- 'If in PCVMode or PSVMode and inspiratoryPauseButton is pressed at any time,' (represents a trigger)
- 'then, at the end of the next inspiration phase': We must latch this requirement upon the next inspiratoryPhaseEnd after the button press.
- 'If inspiratoryPauseButton still true, then both inValveClose and outValveClose must hold': (inValveClose & outValveClose) should be true after the trigger, and remain true UNTIL inspiratoryPauseButton is unpressed.

We model this as:
Globally: Whenever (PCVMode | PSVMode) & X F (inspiratoryPauseButton), then after the next inspiratoryPhaseEnd while inspiratoryPauseButton remains true, inValveClose & outValveClose holds until (inspiratoryPauseButton is released).

But a standard LTL encoding is:
Globally, at any position, if (PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton, then after the next inspiratoryPhaseEnd (while inspiratoryPauseButton still holds), (inValveClose & outValveClose) holds until !inspiratoryPauseButton.

Alternatively, this can be encoded as:

G ( ((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton & X F(inspiratoryPhaseEnd)) -> X ( (inspiratoryPhaseEnd & inspiratoryPauseButton) -> (inValveClose & outValveClose) U (!inspiratoryPauseButton) ) )

But since inspiratoryPhaseEnd is momentary and inspiratoryPauseButton may be pressed across several cycles, a common abstract encoding is:

G ( (PCVMode | PSVMode) & X F (inspiratoryPauseButton & X inspiratoryPhaseEnd) -> X (G ( (inspiratoryPauseButton & inspiratoryPhaseEnd) -> (inValveClose & outValveClose) U (!inspiratoryPauseButton) ) ) )

But for clarity and safety, we can state at every step:
If (PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton, whenever inspiratoryPhaseEnd holds and inspiratoryPauseButton is true, then from then, inValveClose & outValveClose hold until !inspiratoryPauseButton.

So, the final LTL formula is:
G ( ((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton & X inspiratoryPhaseEnd) -> X ( (inspiratoryPauseButton) -> (inValveClose & outValveClose) U (!inspiratoryPauseButton) ) )

This encodes the requirement: In the next state after both conditions, if inspiratoryPauseButton is true, then both valves remain closed until the pause is stopped.

However, for ensuring that this is always possible after each inspiration phase, we make sure to encode this trigger on every next inspiratoryPhaseEnd after the pause button is set.

Explanation steps:
1. Identify mode: (PCVMode | PSVMode)
2. Initiate when Inspiratory Pause Button is pressed: inspiratoryPauseButton
3. Wait for next end of inspiration: next (X) inspiratoryPhaseEnd
4. If inspiratoryPauseButton still true after inspiratoryPhaseEnd, then (inValveClose & outValveClose) holds until inspiratoryPauseButton is released (!inspiratoryPauseButton), encoded using U.
5. Globally (G), this must always be enforced.

explanation_dict.natural_language: PCV and PSV modes
Initiate on inspiratoryPauseButton
After the next end of inspiration
If inspiratoryPauseButton still true, close both valves until pause released
Globally required

output_LTL: G( ((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton & X inspiratoryPhaseEnd) -> X ( inspiratoryPauseButton -> (inValveClose & outValveClose) U (!inspiratoryPauseButton) ) )

explanation:
We need to formalize: In PCV and PSV modes, if the inspiratory pause is set via the GUI (inspiratoryPauseButton), then after the next inspiration phase ends (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), if the inspiratory pause is still required (the button is still set), the inspiratory and expiratory valves (inValveClose and outValveClose) are closed until the pause is stopped (button unset). 

Step-by-step: 
1. 'In PCV and PSV modes' is (PCVMode | PSVMode).
2. 'Inspiratory pause set by GUI' is inspiratoryPauseButton.
3. 'Wait for end of next inspiration phase' is that after inspiratoryPauseButton becomes true, at the next inspiratoryPhaseEnd.
4. 'If still required' means inspiratoryPauseButton must still be true at inspiratoryPhaseEnd.
5. 'Both the inspiratory and expiratory valves close until pause is stopped' means inValveClose & outValveClose holds globally from inspiratoryPhaseEnd (with button set) until inspiratoryPauseButton is false.
6. This is triggered every time after inspiratoryPauseButton becomes true while in the appropriate modes.

This leads to the following LTL:
Globally, whenever ((PCVMode | PSVMode) & X (inspiratoryPauseButton & X F inspiratoryPhaseEnd)), then at that inspiratoryPhaseEnd, if inspiratoryPauseButton still holds, inValveClose and outValveClose remain true until inspiratoryPauseButton becomes false.

However, a more precise and simpler encoding is:
Globally, if (PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton & X F(inspiratoryPhaseEnd & inspiratoryPauseButton), then from that (inspiratoryPhaseEnd & inspiratoryPauseButton) point inValveClose & outValveClose holds until NOT inspiratoryPauseButton.

So, in pseudo-LTL:
G( ((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton & X F (inspiratoryPhaseEnd & inspiratoryPauseButton)) -> (F ((inspiratoryPhaseEnd & inspiratoryPauseButton) & (inValveClose & outValveClose U !inspiratoryPauseButton))) )

But simpler:
Globally: whenever (PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton becomes true, eventually there is a time instant where inspiratoryPhaseEnd & inspiratoryPauseButton, and from then inValveClose & outValveClose holds until inspiratoryPauseButton becomes false.

This can be encoded compactly as:
G( ((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton & X F (inspiratoryPhaseEnd & inspiratoryPauseButton)) -> F ( (inspiratoryPhaseEnd & inspiratoryPauseButton) & ( (inValveClose & outValveClose) U !inspiratoryPauseButton ) ) )

Alternatively, the main essential core property, omitting some corner case repetitions, is:
G( ((PCVMode | PSVMode) & X (inspiratoryPauseButton & X inspiratoryPhaseEnd)) -> X ( G ( ((inspiratoryPhaseEnd & inspiratoryPauseButton) -> ( (inValveClose & outValveClose) U !inspiratoryPauseButton ) ) ) )

But the most direct (and acceptable in this context):
G( ((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton & X inspiratoryPhaseEnd & X inspiratoryPauseButton) -> X ( (inValveClose & outValveClose) U !inspiratoryPauseButton ) )

if Inspiratory Pause still required
